behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded even when they have high relevance scores.
behaviors.justification: Failure commonly appears at module handoff (catalog availability state + ranking pipeline). A pure unit test of ranking or filtering alone can miss incorrect wiring or stale availability flags.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation generation end-to-end in-process with real filter+ranking wiring and a local data store/fixture containing mixed stock states, including a top-scoring out-of-stock item. Assert no out-of-stock IDs appear in final ranked output.

behaviors.description: Blocked user categories are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are often in preference retrieval/mapping into filter logic, not the predicate itself. Mock-only unit tests can hide settings-schema or wiring mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Use real preference-loading + category-filter modules with a fixture user that blocks specific categories. Generate recommendations and verify blocked categories are absent while allowed categories remain.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Ranking uses multiple local signals (history, browsing, similar-user features). Failures often come from feature assembly/weight application across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Build fixtures where two products are similar except one has strong recent browsing signal and the other has none. Run full local ranking pipeline and assert the browsing-matched product is ranked higher.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page with stable ordering across repeated page-2 requests when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: This is a local wiring/state issue (sorting + pagination + repository query semantics). Unit tests can miss unstable ordering introduced by data access layer behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Seed enough products for multiple pages in a local DB/test repository, request page 2 twice with identical inputs, and assert identical ordered IDs and page boundaries.

behaviors.description: Recommendations include at least one product from an unbrowsed category (serendipity constraint).
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-component business rule (candidate generation + diversification/ranking constraints). A unit test can miss whether the rule survives full ranking pipeline composition.
behaviors.test_description: Create user/history fixtures with heavily dominant browsed categories and ensure candidate pool includes other categories. Run full recommendation pipeline and assert at least one result is from an unbrowsed category.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users receive popularity-based recommendations instead of an empty list.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback-path wiring (no-history detection -> popularity source -> ranking output). Mocked units can miss fallback not being invoked end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Use a user fixture with no browsing/purchase history and a populated catalog with popularity scores. Run recommendation generation and assert non-empty results aligned with popularity ordering.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores product ID, rank position, and timestamp correctly for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are typically serialization/schema/field-mapping issues between tracking emitter and persistence layer. Unit mocks can hide incorrect persisted shape.
behaviors.test_description: Emit click events from recommendation UI/service boundary into real local persistence (DB or event log). Assert stored records contain exact product ID, displayed position, and correctly populated timestamp.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation remains under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Latency is sensitive to runtime/OS behavior, real I/O, and process performance characteristics. In-process mocked tests can pass while production-like execution breaches SLA.
behaviors.test_description: Run a performance test in a production-like environment/process with a 100k-product dataset and realistic request mix. Measure end-to-end recommendation latency and assert p95 (or required percentile) stays below 200ms.

behaviors.description: After a user purchases a recommended product, that product is deprioritized in later recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Requires state transition across modules (purchase event ingestion -> profile/state update -> ranking impact). Single-function tests miss cross-module update timing/wiring issues.
behaviors.test_description: Generate baseline recommendations, record rank of a target product, simulate purchase event through real local update path, regenerate recommendations, and assert the purchased product moves down or out per policy.

behaviors.description: Recommendations are deterministic for unchanged user state across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Non-determinism often comes from local module interactions (unstable sort, unordered collections, random seed handling, repository ordering). Unit tests may miss emergent instability.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user state and unchanged underlying data, call recommendation generation twice (or more) via full in-process pipeline and assert identical ordered result IDs each run.
